questions.question: Describe the machine learning process with a detailed explanation of each step.
keyPoints: Problem definition: business objectives, formulate as ML task
Data collection: sources, integration, privacy
Data preparation: cleaning, EDA, feature engineering, selection
Model selection: algorithm choice based on problem type
Model training: split data, train, tune hyperparameters
Model evaluation: metrics (accuracy/MSE/silhouette), compare baselines
Deployment: API, production integration
Maintenance: monitor drift, retrain

questions.question: Explain the data preparation step in the ML process. What sub-activities does it include?
keyPoints: Data cleaning: missing values, duplicates, errors
EDA: distributions, correlations, visualizations
Feature engineering: create new features from existing data
Feature selection: choose relevant features (PCA, correlation)
Data transformation: scaling, normalization, encoding

questions.question: Why is data split into training, validation, and test sets? Explain the purpose of each.
keyPoints: Training set: used to train the model
Validation set: used for hyperparameter tuning
Test set: final unbiased evaluation on unseen data
Typical ratios: 70/15/15 or 60/20/20
Prevents overfitting to any single dataset

questions.question: What is model deployment and maintenance? Explain concept drift.
keyPoints: Deployment: packaging model as API, integrating with production
Maintenance: monitoring, retraining, updating
Concept drift: target variable relationship changes over time
Data drift: input data distribution changes
Need periodic retraining to maintain accuracy

questions.question: List the evaluation metrics for classification and regression problems.
keyPoints: Classification: accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC-ROC
Regression: MSE, RMSE, MAE, R-squared
Clustering: silhouette score, inertia
